headline: Maternal Mental Health Screening Rate Only 18% Postpartum
context: Fewer than 1 in 5 new mothers receive postnatal mental health screening despite high rates of postpartum depression.

cards.big: Only 18% of new mothers screened for postpartum depression at 6-week checkup
cards.sub: Estimated 22% of Malaysian mothers experience postnatal depression

cards.big: Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale takes 5 minutes but is not part of standard protocol
cards.sub: Simple validated screening tool exists but is not mandated in postnatal care

cards.big: Untreated maternal depression affects child development for first 1,000 days of life
cards.sub: Maternal mental health directly impacts infant attachment and cognitive growth

cards.big: Scotland mandates universal postnatal mental health screening achieving 94% coverage since 2019
cards.sub: Mandatory protocol integration into routine postnatal visits drives near-universal screening

cards.big: Ignoring maternal mental health harms two people at once—the mother and her child
Should postnatal mental health screening be mandatory at all government clinic checkups
